items.title: The dog that didn’t bark: What the Danish election reveals about Russian influence operations
items.summary: According to Danish state authorities, there were no major foreign influence campaigns to speak of in the lead-up to Denmark’s March 2026 parliamentary election. Local fact-checkers and journalists tracked pro-Russian narratives and fringe propaganda channels, but nothing resembling a broad, coordinated campaign with significant reach emerged. Monitoring and analysis by Defense Innovation Highway and OpenMinds of part of the Danish online information environment during the campaign reached similar conclusions. The post The dog that didn’t bark: What the Danish election reveals about Russian influence operations appeared first on EUvsDisinfo .
source_names: EUvsDisinfo
related_titles: The dog that didn’t bark: What the Danish election reveals about Russian influence operations

items.title: Latvian house prices continue to surge
items.summary: In the first quarter of 2026, house prices, as measured by the House Price Index, increased by 4.7% in the euro area and by 5.1% in the EU compared with the same quarter of the previous year. In the fourth quarter of 2025, house prices increased by 5.1% and 5.4% in the euro area and EU, respectively. These figures come from Eurostat, the statistical office of the European Union and were published on July 2nd.
source_names: LSM English Latvia
related_titles: Latvian house prices continue to surge

items.summary: Estonia's technical education is improving and young people's interest is rising quickly, but this must be matched by employers' effort — companies must take responsibility for ensuring that motivated, trained young people find their place in industry. items.title: New car market on the decline in Latvia

items.title: Enhanced border surveillance extended in Latvia
items.summary: The enhanced border security has been extended until December 31 of this year in Ludza Municipality, Krāslava Municipality, Augšdaugava Municipality, the city of Daugavpils, and Kaunata Parish in Rēzekne Municipality, as a large number of border violators continues to be observed along the Latvian–Belarusian border, the government decided on July 2.
source_names: LSM English Latvia
related_titles: Enhanced border surveillance extended in Latvia

events.title: The dog that didn’t bark: What the Danish election reveals about Russian influence operations
events.summary: According to Danish state authorities, there were no major foreign influence campaigns to speak of in the lead-up to Denmark’s March 2026 parliamentary election. Local fact-checkers and journalists tracked pro-Russian narratives and fringe propaganda channels, but nothing resembling a broad, coordinated campaign with significant reach emerged. Monitoring and analysis by Defense Innovation Highway and OpenMinds of part of the Danish online information environment during the campaign reached similar conclusions. The post The dog that didn’t bark: What the Danish election reveals about Russian influence operations appeared first on EUvsDisinfo .
source_names: EUvsDisinfo
related_titles: The dog that didn’t bark: What the Danish election reveals about Russian influence operations

events.title: New customs rules aim to counter shoddy imports into EU
events.summary: New customs procedures for low-value shipments from third countries entered into force in the European Union from July 1st. The main aim of the changes is to ensure fairer competition conditions for European companies, improve consumer safety and reduce the uncontrolled influx of cheap goods of low quality.
source_names: LSM English Latvia
related_titles: New customs rules aim to counter shoddy imports into EU

events.title: Latvian businesses urged to apply for EU defence funding
events.summary: The Latvian Ministry of Defence is inviting defence industry companies to prepare and submit applications for European Defence Industry Programme (EDIP) funding in a timely manner.
source_names: LSM English Latvia
related_titles: Latvian businesses urged to apply for EU defence funding

events.title: Latvian house prices continue to surge
events.summary: In the first quarter of 2026, house prices, as measured by the House Price Index, increased by 4.7% in the euro area and by 5.1% in the EU compared with the same quarter of the previous year. In the fourth quarter of 2025, house prices increased by 5.1% and 5.4% in the euro area and EU, respectively. These figures come from Eurostat, the statistical office of the European Union and were published on July 2nd.
source_names: LSM English Latvia
related_titles: Latvian house prices continue to surge

events.title: Uncertainty remains over delayed U.S. weapons deliveries to Estonia
events.summary: Estonian Defense Minister Hanno Pevkur says U.S. weapons shipments to Estonia are on their way, but he still cannot give exact dates. Matthew E. Wall, deputy chief of mission at the U.S. Embassy, likewise did not mention any timelines, stressing instead that the key issue is greater allied contributions to NATO's defense capability.

events.title: Andri Haran: Employers must take responsibility for bringing young people into industry
events.summary: Estonia's technical education is improving and young people's interest is rising quickly, but this must be matched by employers' effort — companies must take responsibility for ensuring that motivated, trained young people find their place in industry. Otherwise Estonia will remain stuck at Europe's middle‑income level, writes Andri Haran.
source_names: ERR News Estonia
related_titles: Andri Haran: Employers must take responsibility for bringing young people into industry

events.title: Alūksne hosts European Speedboat Championship

related_titles: Alūksne hosts European Speedboat Championship

events.title: Government moves to restrict property ownership by Russian and Belarusian citizens

related_titles: Government moves to restrict property ownership by Russian and Belarusian citizens

events.title: New car market on the decline in Latvia
events.summary: The European Automobile Manufacturers’ Association, which brings together virtually all automakers operating in our part of the world, recently published data on the new car market in Europe, showing that Latvia is one of nine countries in Europe where the new car market has declined, LSM's Māris Bergs reports on July 3.
source_names: LSM English Latvia
related_titles: New car market on the decline in Latvia

events.title: Enhanced border surveillance extended in Latvia
events.summary: The enhanced border security has been extended until December 31 of this year in Ludza Municipality, Krāslava Municipality, Augšdaugava Municipality, the city of Daugavpils, and Kaunata Parish in Rēzekne Municipality, as a large number of border violators continues to be observed along the Latvian–Belarusian border, the government decided on July 2.
source_names: LSM English Latvia
related_titles: Enhanced border surveillance extended in Latvia
